step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the provided rationale but add another perspective on why beta-1,3-glucan isn't relevant for diagnosing Pneumocystis carinii infections specifically. While all mentioned fungal infections lack substantial β-1,3-glucan, Pneumocystis carinii has unique cyst wall proteins that make direct antigen tests more appropriate than β-1,3-glucan assays for its identification.

**My final answer remains:** D.
